Notice Of Motion To Compel Cross-Defendant Gary Lius Further Response To Request For Production, Set One, And Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $7,500.00 Against Gary Liu And His Attorneys Of Record
Off Calendar Jul-16-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT LIBERTY MUTUAL INSURANCE COMPANY, THE OHIO CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion To Compel Cross-Defendant Gary Liu's Further Response To Request For Production, Set One, And Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $7,500.00 Against Gary Liu And His Attorneys Of Record:

Ohio Casualty Insurance Company's "motion to compel cross-defendant Gary Liu's further response to request for production, set one" is denied. The judge pro tem's report and recommendation are adopted. I share the judge pro tem's "concern that the information sought may not lead to the discovery of admissible evidence in the bad faith case. Rather, it appears to be a costly and time consuming effort to require Oak Trucking and Mr. Liu to organize, sort and categorize documents which are already in the possession, custody or control of the moving party." No sanctions are awarded against anyone.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Laura Reding, CSR No. 9711, Email: lreding@sftc.org. =(302/RBU)

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANTs And Cross Complainants LIBERTY MUTUAL INSURANCE COMPANY, and THE OHIO CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion For An Order Of Contempt And Imposition Of All Appropriate Sanctions Against Subpoenaed Nonparty Law Firms Re Failure To Comply With December 23, 2019 Order

Defendant and Cross-Complainant The Ohio Casualty Company's motion for an order of contempt and imposition of all appropriate sanctions against subpoenaed nonparty law firms re failure to comply with December 23, 2019 order is granted in part. To the extent they have not already done so, within 25 days of entry of the court's order, the nonparty firms shall produce all outstanding responsive non-privileged documents required by the court's December 23, 2019 order, together with a verified response by the firm's custodian of records affirming that a diligent search and a reasonable inquiry has been made in an attempt to comply with the subpoenas. The documents to be produced shall include (a) all documents withheld based on mediation confidentiality created after October 3, 2018, and (b) all documents related to the Avalos declaration, including drafts of that declaration and related emails and other communications. By the same date, the responding firms that have not already done so shall serve privilege logs listing the documents or categories of documents withheld on the basis of mediation confidentiality, the attorney-client privilege, the attorney work-product doctrine, or any other applicable privilege, in sufficient factual detail to enable any claim of privilege to be adjudicated. Defendant's requests for contempt and monetary sanctions are denied.

order is signed.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-16-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF SECURITY NATIONAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE AMENDED COMPLAINT.

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff Security National Insurance Company's motion for leave to file first amended complaint is granted. There is a liberal policy to allow amendments and any failure of plaintiff to comply with the letter of CRC 3.1324 did not prejudice defendants given defendants' comprehensive opposition to the instant motion. The court agrees that it can deny leave to amend where a claim fails as a matter of law. Nevertheless, given the numerous issues raised by the proposed amendments and defendants' opposition, the court determines that the wiser course of action is to allow amendment. Defendants are free to test the sufficiency of the amended complaint by demurrer, motion for judgment on the pleadings or other appropriate proceedings. (See Brown v. Ralphs Grocery Co. (2018) 28 Cal.App.5th 824, 843 ["The 'better course of action' is to allow a plaintiff to amend the complaint 'and then let the parties test its legal sufficiency in other appropriate proceedings.'"].) Defendants have sufficient time to do so given the current trial date of February 16, 2021. Order signed. Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, (E: maria.torreano@gmail.com) (302/EPS)

MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-19-572725 WITH CASE CGC-18-564705
Off Calendar Aug-23-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Whitney A. Davis, Esq. of K.W. Davis, T: 916.333.5363, for plaintiff & moving party;
Jennifer Hoffman, Esq. of Sheppard Mullin, T:213.617.4136, for defendant & opposing party Ohio Casualty;
Laura R. Grealish, Esq. of Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeney, T:415.962.2814, for defendant JLIS.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested. Defendants argue that consolidating now would be premature. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF OAK TRUCKING, LLC'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTION OF CASE NO. CGC-19-572725 WITH CASE NO. CGC-18-564705 as follows:

Plaintiff Oak Trucking, LLC's motion to consolidate matters is GRANTED. Under Code of Civil Procedure section 1048, the Court has broad discretion to consolidate actions with common questions of law or fact. (National Elec. Supply Co. v. Mt. Diablo Unified School Dist. (1960) 187 Cal.App.2d 418, 421.) Although Defendant JLIS Inc.'s connection to the bad faith case (No. CGC-19-572725) is tenuous given the conclusory conspiracy allegations, the legal issue of whether the policy issued by defendants Liberty Mutual Insurance Company and Ohio Casualty Insurance Company obligated them to provide coverage for plaintiff Glenn F. Pasiliao's injury and/or defend Oak Trucking is intertwined with the adequacy of coverage procured by JLIS in the broker negligence case (No. CGC-18-564705). The risk of inconsistent rulings on this key issue in the two cases if left unconsolidated outweighs the potential for delay and jury confusion. Defendants have not shown substantial prejudice and judicial economy will be promoted by avoiding duplication of discovery. The cases shall be consolidated for all purposes with case number CGC-18-564705 as the lead case.

Order signed in open court.
